author: garyrong
gurukamath
content:
If the transaction OOGs here, I believe it is to be interpreted as an Exceptional halt. I remember this was part of the EIP text at one point but might have been deleted by accident.
<@968526635735326741> I think it might be worth adding back the OOG scenario explicitly in this [line](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/blob/34f7405965ea5cc7135050e1741d903971a1fb6b/EIPS/eip-2780.md?plain=1#L85), along with contract creating tx initcode revert.

author: gurukamath
content: The whole idea behind splitting the gas charges into intrinsic gas + later charge for new account creation/ delegation access, was to allow transactions to be validated/invalidated without needing further state access

author: misilva73
content: Agreed. I will update the EIP

author: draganrakita
content: For confirmation, spec (and tests) are still doing eip7702 refund to the reservoir, and not doing source based refund, right?

author: chfast
content: I have a question why the access list doesn't worm the 7702 delegations? See  test `test_top_frame_charges_delegation_in_access_list`.

author: gurukamath
content: The delegation is in the access list, but the top level frame continues to charge cold access cost. See [here](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/blob/34f7405965ea5cc7135050e1741d903971a1fb6b/EIPS/eip-2780.md?plain=1#L80)
